Observed potentially hazardous food (PHF) items cold held at the required temperature of 45F or below such as hard boiled eggs (actual 45F) in the two door sliding glass door commercial refrigeration unit and raw chicken breast (actual 40F) in the walk-in cooler. An analog probe thermometer was available to ensure PHF items are cooked to the minimum required internal temperature and or hot held at the minimum required temperature of 140F or above. No PHF items were being cooked and or hot held at the time of the inspection. The facility is equipped with a three bay sink utilized as a hand washing station and to wash, rinse and sanitize dishware/ utensils. Sanitarian advises the facility to utilize the first bay of the sink as a hand washing station during food service operations and after food service operations use the first bay to wash dishware/ utensils, use the second bay to rinse dishware/ utensils and utilize the third bay to sanitize dishware/ utensils. The facility also has a dishwasher available. Sanitarian advised the operator gloves should be changed in between tasks; such as, staff should change gloves after changing tasks/ activities and or handling money before returning back to food preparation. Sanitarian discussed the approved method to cool food items, uncovered under commercial refrigeration at a depth of no more than four inches and to follow the cooling schedule of 120F to 70F in two hours and 70F to 45F in an additional four hours for a total cooling schedule of six hours. Sanitarian provided the operator with the cooling schedule educational material for reference, recommended food storage guidance and provided a food safety summary reference sheet at the time of the inspection.
